articles.title: This Week's Market Wrap: Narrow Leadership, Oil And China Trip, And Hot Inflation
articles.excerpt: Strong earnings and guidance from companies tied to AI infrastructure, networking, semiconductors, and data centers pushed major indices to fresh record highs, with companies like Cisco Systems, Nvidia, Applied Materials, and Broadcom leading the momentum trade. Escalating tensio

articles.title: FDA shuffles top drug, biologics leaders in latest shakeup
articles.excerpt: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration is shuffling its top drug and biologics regulators, according to a memo seen by CNBC. The shake up caps a tumultuous week at the FDA.

articles.title: Nasdaq Gets Extended
articles.excerpt: The sharp rally off of the March 30th low has seen the Nasdaq climb 25.9%. In percentage terms, the Nasdaq was down 8.4% below that same moving average, which was a lot, but far from any sort of record-setting reading.

articles.title: Striking Breadth Levels
articles.excerpt: The S&P 500 has been driven higher by large-cap hyperscalers and semiconductors over the last month. The index would need to fall 8% just to get back down to its 50-DMA from these levels.

articles.title: Review & Preview: Even AI Isn't Forever
articles.excerpt: All three major indexes stumbled on Friday as inflation worries and geopolitical concerns dampened market optimism.

articles.title: Korean Stocks Are Up 89%. Hedge Funders Suggest How to Play It.
articles.excerpt: South Korea is often-overlooked but its stock market is the hottest in the world this year. The boom is led by chip powerhouses SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics.

articles.title: Jim Cramer: Why the bond market has become a thorn in the market's side
articles.excerpt: CNBC's Jim Cramer warned that rising bond yields could threaten the stock market rally and reduce the chances of interest rate cuts. He turned to the week ahead, highlighting reports from Nvidia, retailers and housing-related names.

articles.title: John Delaney's Forbright files for US IPO
articles.excerpt: American lender Forbright on Friday filed ​for an initial public ‌offering in the United States.

articles.title: Friday's Final Takeaways: Breakthroughs in Trump's China Visit
articles.excerpt: Sam Vadas turns to China for Friday's final takeaways, offering her perspective on the key U.S. victories she sees in President Trump's overseas trip.

articles.title: Short Selling And Put Buying Point To Continuing Rally
articles.excerpt: Short selling and put buying indicators continue to signal further upside for the technology sector rally. Current levels of bearish sentiment, measured by short fund activity and tech put buying, remain elevated and not yet contrarian-bearish.

articles.title: The Federal Reserve said that outgoing Chair Jerome Powell would remain as its acting leader until Kevin Warsh, who was confirmed to a four-year term by the Senate this week, could be sworn in
articles.excerpt: The Federal Reserve said that outgoing Chair Jerome Powell would remain as its acting leader until Kevin Warsh, who was confirmed to a four-year term by the Senate this week, could be sworn in.

articles.title: Equities & Bonds Take a Hit To Close the Week | Closing Bell
articles.excerpt: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets Like this video?

articles.title: Mounting Inflation Pressures Deepen Global Bond Slide
articles.excerpt: The yield on the 10-year Treasury climbed to nearly 4.6% Friday, its highest level in more than a year.

articles.title: The Soaring Stock Market Is Just Getting Started, Don't Miss Out
articles.excerpt: What a year it's been so far. YTD, the S&P 500 is up 8.22%, and the Nasdaq is up 12.8%. And the year is not even half over yet. What a difference a couple of months can make.

articles.title: Stocks tumble as Treasury yields spike and Iran fears shake markets
articles.excerpt: The selloff gathered steam after the 30-year Treasury yield shot above 5.1%, approaching levels not seen in nearly two decades.

articles.title: Dow slides 537 points as rising oil prices rattle AI-fueled rally
articles.excerpt: US stocks fell sharply on Friday as rising Treasury yields, surging oil prices, and fading enthusiasm surrounding artificial intelligence stocks pressured markets following weeks of record-setting gains. The Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped roughly 537 points, or about 1.1%,

articles.title: Optical stocks are booming. Here's how to invest in one of the most explosive areas of tech.
articles.excerpt: Investors are pouring money quickly into a new ETF that invests in companies involved with photonics and photolithography.

articles.title: When one Powell closes, another Warsh opens
articles.excerpt: Jerome Powell's time leading the Federal Reserve is officially coming to an end. We look back at some of the defining moments of Powell's tenure, from the pandemic and emergency rate cuts to inflation, banking turmoil, and growing political scrutiny surrounding the Fed.

articles.title: Grading Powell's Fed: Good for Stocks, Bad for Affordability
articles.excerpt: One result of the massive monetary expansion was an upsurge in inflation, to a peak of over 9% in 2022—which the Fed infamously termed “transitory.”

articles.title: Wall Street Prices Out Rate Cuts, Eyes Hikes, Global Bond Selloff Deepens | Real Yield 5/15/2026
articles.excerpt: "Bloomberg Real Yield" highlights the market-moving news you need to know. Today's guests: Columbia Threadneedle Portfolio Manager, Total Return Bond Ed Al-Hussainy, JPMorgan Management CIO of US GFICC Kay Herr, CreditSights Global Head of Credit Strategy Winnie Cisar, and Ironsi
